mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals an impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower future cash flows and generating negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal exhibits no predictive power over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based demand expansion, reducing recession risk and boosting equity risk appetite over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or equity returns are negative when both series expand simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during post-shock recovery phases indicates supply chain normalization and capacity utilization improvement, driving short-term equity outperformance as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: The direction flips to negative during supply-constrained regimes, or industrial production growth shows zero correlation with 5-day forward equity returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding a 3% annualized growth rate forces market participants to price in aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite sustained high inflation readings, or the signal exhibits a positive Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where retail sales growth outpaces real GDP growth signals a consumer-driven economic expansion, supporting equity valuations over a quarterly horizon as corporate revenue forecasts rise.
failure_condition: Consumer spending outpaces GDP growth but equity returns are negative, or the interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month employment growth reduces recession probabilities and supports corporate earnings trajectories, driving positive short-term equity momentum across all market environments.
failure_condition: Employment growth correlates negatively with 5-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.8 million units annually during low-rate environments signal robust credit conditions and builder confidence, driving positive equity returns over the next month as financial conditions ease.
failure_condition: High housing starts fail to predict positive equity returns when credit spreads widen, or the forward return distribution shows no significant difference between triggered and non-triggered periods.
